articles.title: Underdog Cuts Jobs While Pivoting to Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Underdog Fantasy is laying off employees as it shifts focus from daily fantasy sports to prediction markets. The move signals another major player entering the space, which could bring fresh capital and mainstream legitimacy to political and event betting.

articles.title: Polymarket user wins $200,000 betting on date of Israeli attack of Iran
articles.summary: A Polymarket trader correctly predicted the timing of Israel's retaliatory strike on Iran and walked away with $200,000. The win demonstrates how geopolitical prediction markets can reward those who successfully navigate the intersection of military intelligence, diplomatic signals, and timing speculation.

articles.title: Kalshi points to ‘death carveout’ in Khamenei market as punters revolt
articles.summary: Kalshi traders are pushing back against the platform's handling of its Khamenei succession market, with the company pointing to fine print that excludes death-related outcomes from payouts. The revolt highlights how murky contract terms can blow up in prediction markets' faces, especially when big political events don't fit neatly into preset categories.

articles.title: Kalshi licenses election data from the AP
articles.summary: Kalshi has struck a deal with the Associated Press to use their election data, likely for settling election betting markets on their platform. This gives Kalshi access to AP's gold standard election calls, which should speed up market resolution and boost credibility with bettors who want fast, authoritative settlements.

articles.title: Prediction markets point to Talarico-Paxton showdown in Texas Senate race
articles.summary: Prediction markets are signaling that Democrat James Talarico and Republican Angela Paxton will face off in a key Texas Senate race, suggesting these two have emerged as the clear frontrunners. This matchup matters because Texas races often see wild swings in betting odds as demographic shifts collide with traditional red-state assumptions, creating profit opportunities for sharp bettors who can read the state's evolving political landscape.

articles.title: Indian Gaming Association Condemns CFTC Over Prediction Markets, Warns of Threat to Tribal Sovereignty
articles.summary: The Indian Gaming Association is pushing back against the CFTC's expanded oversight of prediction markets, arguing it threatens tribal nations' sovereign right to regulate gaming on their lands. This could fracture the regulatory landscape if tribes decide to launch their own prediction markets under tribal gaming laws, creating a patchwork of jurisdictions that traders would need to navigate.

articles.title: There's Lots Of Noise About Prediction Markets In Congress. But Will Anything Happen?
articles.summary: Congress is making noise about regulating prediction markets, but the actual legislative prospects remain murky. Any real action could reshape how Americans bet on politics and current events, making this more than just political theater for the industry.

articles.title: A Former Top Trump Official Is Going After Prediction Markets
articles.summary: A former senior Trump administration official is launching some kind of challenge or criticism against prediction markets. This could signal incoming regulatory pressure or political scrutiny that might shake up how these platforms operate in the US.

articles.title: Prediction market bets on Iran strikes stoke insider trading, ethics scrutiny
articles.summary: Regulators are examining prediction markets that allowed betting on Iran-related events, likely around military strikes or political developments in the Middle East. The scrutiny signals authorities are getting serious about which geopolitical events should be fair game for betting, potentially reshaping what markets can legally offer.

articles.title: NBA star's partnership with a prediction market raises new questions for a troubled sports betting landscape
articles.summary: An NBA player has partnered with a prediction market platform, adding another layer of complexity to sports betting's already messy regulatory and ethical landscape. The move signals prediction markets are aggressively courting mainstream sports figures, but it also hands regulators and leagues fresh ammunition to crack down on an industry they're already eyeing suspiciously.

articles.title: Polymarket defends its decision to allow betting on war as ‘invaluable’
articles.summary: Polymarket is pushing back against criticism of its war-related betting markets, calling them "invaluable" for information discovery. The defense signals prediction markets are doubling down on controversial topics despite regulatory pressure, potentially setting the stage for broader fights over what can and can't be traded.

articles.title: MrBeast video editor fined over insider trading
articles.summary: A video editor working for MrBeast got slapped with fines for trading on inside information about upcoming content or business moves. This shows how prediction markets around YouTuber announcements and creator economy bets are getting serious enough that regulators are now watching for information leaks from production teams.
